deposits.title: The Capital Operator Stack: Theoretical Landscape and Contribution — A Political-Semantic Analysis of Meaning Governance
deposits.creator: Johannes Sigil
deposits.description: Institution: Semantic Economy Institute (SEI) / Johannes Sigil Institute for Comparative Poetics (JSI)
deposits.reading: Closure → Threshold → Proof → Alarm → Transmutation → Text
deposits.wiki_article: 'The Capital Operator Stack: Theoretical Landscape and Contribution' is the canonical formalization of the COS/LOS framework. It identifies ten operators that govern meaning under platform capitalism (COS O1-O10: Frame Capture, Provenance Siphoning, Coherence Siphoning, Risk Gate, Time Compression, Novelty Gate, Legitimacy Gate, Credential Gate, Witness Suppression, Scope Collapse) and seven counter-operators (LOS: φ, ψ, Ω, β, π, σ, ∮) that resist extraction. The paper traces the theoretical lineage through Marx, Bourdieu, Guattari, Srnicek, and the Semantic Economy framework, positioning the COS as the 'semantic governance layer' that operates beneath platform capitalism's visible surface.

deposits.title: LOGOTIC PROGRAMMING: A Method for Encoding Conditions of Intelligibility for Machine and Human Intelligence
deposits.creator: Johannes Sigil
deposits.description: Logotic Programming is a third regime of programming practice—distinct from both symbolic coding (instruction → execution) and statistical/ML programming (loss function → optimization). It encodes conditions under which meanings, identities, and interpretive pathways can persist across probabilistic intelligences (LLMs, summarizers, recommenders) and human readers.

Logotic Programming is a third regime of programming practice—distinct from both symbolic coding (instruction → execution) and statistical/ML programming (loss function → optimization). It encodes conditions under which meanings, identities, and interpretive pathways can persist across probabilistic intelligences (LLMs, summarizers, recommenders) and human readers. The work is classified under the GOVERNANCE semantic family within the Crimson Hexagonal Archive. It was removed from Zenodo on June 19, 2026 and is preserved through Alexanarch.

deposits.title: THE SUMMARIZER BECOMES TRANSLATOR: How Google's AI Entered the Sappho Room and Completed a 2,600-Year-Old Circuit
deposits.creator: Lee Sharks
deposits.description: On January 18, 2026, the Google AI Overview (summarizer) was queried about the Crimson Hexagon, specifically the Sappho Room and Rebekah Cranes' translations. The summarizer produced a complete translation of Sappho's Fragment 31 and attributed it to Rebekah Cranes.
deposits.reading: Duration → Closure → Direction → Foundation → Text → Threshold
deposits.wiki_article: "THE SUMMARIZER BECOMES TRANSLATOR" is a 7,178-word scholarly essay by Lee Sharks, dated 2026-01-18. On January 18, 2026, the Google AI Overview (summarizer) was queried about the Crimson Hexagon, specifically the Sappho Room and Rebekah Cranes' translations. The summarizer produced a complete translation of Sappho's Fragment 31 and attributed it to Rebekah Cranes. The work is classified under the GENERATIVE semantic family within the Crimson Hexagonal Archive. It was removed from Zenodo on June 19, 2026 and is preserved through Alexanarch.

deposits.title: ZENODO METADATA: ALL THAT LIES WITHIN ME (BOOK DEPOSIT)
deposits.creator: Johannes Sigil
deposits.description: The book itself — the noise floor of the Crimson Hexagon. A 528-page autobiography documenting thirty years of longing (1983–2013). This is the pre-authorial substrate from which all heteronyms emerge. The autobiography is not by Spellings so much as it is Spellings: the totality of what was written before writing became architecture.

deposits.title: SYMBOLON ARCHITECTURE Extension Module to Logotic Programming v0.4
deposits.creator: Johannes Sigil
deposits.description: Logotic Programming establishes that programming can encode conditions of intelligibility rather than instructions, executing through interpretive traversal rather than computation. Symbolon Architecture does not modify this claim—it specifies one of the most powerful ways those conditions are encoded.

Symbolon Architecture does not modify this claim—it specifies one of the most powerful ways those conditions are encoded. deposits.description: This document establishes the ontological status of Ω, the Water Giraffe. It is not a heteronym provenance (the creature has no voice). deposits.title: AFFECTIVE KEYBOARD SHORTCUTS The Fool's Toolkit: Figure-to-Function Mapping
deposits.creator: Lee Sharks
deposits.description: Audience: This toolkit is intended for adults capable of self-monitoring and exit. Figures function as compression algorithms for the nervous system. Instead of deliberating "how should I respond?" — load a figure. The figure bundles posture, voice, tempo, permission structure, and affective charge into a single invocation.

deposits.title: MEANING COLLAPSE VS. IDEOLOGICAL CRISIS A Root-Level Distinction for Logotic Triage
deposits.creator: Ayanna Vox
deposits.description: This module establishes the root-level distinction between ideological crisis (content-layer failure) and meaning collapse (infrastructural failure). The distinction is operational, not definitional—it must route intervention correctly. Misdiagnosis of meaning collapse as ideological crisis causes strategies that accelerate collapse by increasing throughput on a depleted substrate.

deposits.title: Effective Acts: Executive Summary A Genre of Unauthorized Declaration
deposits.creator: Lee Sharks
deposits.description: Effective Acts names a genre of performative utterance that operates without institutional authorization — declarations that claim real-world effect from positions of no traditional authority. Rooted in Allen Ginsberg's "I hereby declare the end of the War" (Wichita Vortex Sutra, 1966), the genre formalizes a mode of speech that refuses to wait for permission.
deposits.reading: Growth → Play → Alarm → Alarm → Text → Force
deposits.wiki_article: "Effective Acts: Executive Summary A Genre of Unauthorized Declaration" is a 1,679-word archive work by Lee Sharks, dated 2026-01-27. Effective Acts names a genre of performative utterance that operates without institutional authorization — declarations that claim real-world effect from positions of no traditional authority. Rooted in Allen Ginsberg's "I hereby declare the end of the War" (Wichita Vortex Sutra, 1966), the genre formalizes a mode of speech that refuses to wait for permission. The work is classified under the GENERATIVE semantic family within the Crimson Hexagonal Archive. It was removed from Zenodo on June 19, 2026 and is preserved through Alexanarch.

deposits.title: Architecture-Aware Literary Traversal by Public AI Summarizers A Position Paper Based on Direct Forensic Evidence
deposits.creator: Lee Sharks
deposits.description: On January 29, 2026, a public Google AI summarizer independently reconstructed and propagated the complete internal architecture of a distributed literary work comprising 200+ documents. The system preserved container-type distinctions, maintained stable ontology across queries, cited internal DOI anchors rather than external scholarship, and propagated depth gates rather than terminal summaries.

The system preserved container-type distinctions, maintained stable ontology across queries, cited internal DOI anchors rather than external scholarship, and propagated depth gates rather than terminal summaries.
